news.headline: Kroger's Q3 earnings show EPS of 98 cents, sales of $33.634 billion (down 0.9%), and LIFO charge of $4 million. CEO mentions confidence in future amidst uncertain economy.
news.summary: Kroger Company (NYSE:KR) stocks are down in premarket trading on Thursday following the release of their third-quarter financial results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 98 cents, meeting expectations. Quarterly sales totaled $33.634 billion, a 0.9% decrease compared to last year, falling short of the projected $34.204 billion. However, Identical Sales ex-fuel rose by 2.3%. The LIFO charge for the quarter was $4 million, significantly lower than the $29 million reported in the same period last year, attributed to lower anticipated inflation. Chairman and CEO Rodney McMullen expressed confidence in the company's ability to navigate the current uncertain economic climate and continue delivering value to customers while investing in their employees.

news.headline: Science Applications International Corp reported 4.3% revenue growth to $1.98 billion, with adjusted EPS of $2.61 in Q3, approving $1.2 billion stock buyback.
news.summary: Science Applications International Corp (SAIC) announced a 4.3% increase in revenue for the fiscal third quarter of 2025, reaching $1.98 billion, surpassing the estimated $1.93 billion. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $2.61 also exceeded expectations, which were set at $2.16. This growth was driven by a rise in contract volumes, both existing and new. Operating margin saw a 50 basis points increase to 9.9%, while the adjusted EBITDA margin rose by 70 basis points to 10.0%. SAIC also achieved $9 million in free cash flow during this quarter. In other news, Amazon has entered the AI supercomputers and servers market to compete with Nvidia by offering more affordable options.

news.headline: Signet Jewelers stock plummet as Q3 earnings show sales dropped 3.1% to $1.35B, missing estimates. EPS at $0.24 missed at $0.33.
news.summary: Signet Jewelers Ltd (NYSE:SIG) saw a significant drop in its stock on Thursday following the release of its fiscal third-quarter 2025 earnings report. The company reported a 3.1% year-on-year decline in sales, totaling $1.35 billion, which fell short of the analyst consensus of $1.37 billion. The adjusted earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $0.24, missing the analyst estimate of $0.33.The quarter also saw a 0.7% decrease in overall same-store sales, with the North American segment experiencing a 0.8% decline while the International segment saw a 1.6% increase in same-store sales. Sales for the North America segment totaled $1.3 billion, representing a 2.3% decrease year-on-year, while International sales plummeted by 11.4% compared to the previous year.In other news, Mastercard recently settled an antitrust lawsuit in the UK for $250 million. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dollar General reports Q3 sales of $10.2 billion, up 5% with same-store sales increasing by 1.3%, despite financial constraints.
news.summary: Dollar General Corporation (NYSE:DG) released its third-quarter results, with an adjusted EPS of $0.89, marking a 29.4% decrease compared to the previous year and falling short of the consensus of $0.94. The company reported quarterly sales of $10.2 billion, surpassing the consensus of $10.14 billion. Sales saw a 5% increase, propelled by new stores and growth in same-store sales, despite the negative impact of store closures. Same-store sales rose by 1.3%, driven by a 1.1% increase in average transaction amount and a 0.3% rise in customer traffic. Notably, consumables saw growth while home, seasonal, and apparel categories experienced declines. Dollar General's Chief, Todd Vasos, expressed satisfaction with the team's performance in the face of challenges like hurricanes that affected business operations.

news.headline: Summary: Exploring the concept of tariffs, their purpose, and impact, particularly in Trump's trade wars. President Trump's stance on tariffs.
news.summary: A tariff is a tax imposed on imported goods to increase their price and make domestic products more competitive. The primary purpose of tariffs is to protect domestic industries from foreign competition and promote economic growth. Ultimately, the burden of tariffs falls on consumers, who end up paying higher prices for goods subject to tariffs. President Trump, known as the "Tariff Man," has implemented various trade tariffs in an effort to reduce the trade deficit and bolster American manufacturing. The impact of these trade wars includes increased costs for businesses and consumers, disruptions in global supply chains, and retaliatory tariffs from other countries. The ongoing debate surrounding tariffs and their effects on the economy continues to be a significant issue in global trade relations.

news.headline: MassageLuXe plans to expand to Grand Rapids, Michigan, known as the state's second-largest city. The franchise offers massage, facial, and waxing services, with high quality as its focus. (Word count: 32)
news.summary: A well-known spa franchise is targeting the bustling second-largest city in Michigan for its expansion endeavors. news.summary: In the upcoming Guild Wars Season 2: Spore Sports event on the Ronin network, the Ethereum farming game Pixels will be distributing PIXEL tokens valued at $4 million.

news.headline: DC advisors increasingly recommend retirement income solutions to help participants in draw-down years. 30% currently recommend (up from 21% in 2022), while another 40% plan to do so in the future. No clear front-runner product, indicating continued industry innovation required.
news.summary: Retirement plan advisors are increasingly recommending retirement income solutions to help plan participants during draw-down years. Currently, 30% of defined contribution (DC) advisors are actively advocating for these solutions, up from 21% in 2022, and another 40% are likely to do so in the future. The lack of a clear front-runner service or product signals the need for ongoing industry innovation. These insights are from the Retirement Plan Advisor Trends™ report by Escalent, which analyzes the behaviors of financial advisors supporting DC retirement plans. The study highlights the growing demand for retirement income solutions as Baby Boomers retire and Gen Xers face financial challenges. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

This tool consolidates online community management into a singular platform, streamlining the process. It marks a significant shift in social media strategy, offering a comprehensive solution.

news.headline: MAIN is revolutionizing consumer data with Zero-Party Data amid FTC scrutiny, addressing unethical data practices.
news.summary: Main Assets & Identity Network (MAIN) from New York is revolutionizing the consumer data marketplace with its Zero-Party Data (ZPD) protocol. Launched on December 5, 2024, this innovative solution comes as a response to increased concerns over unethical data practices. MAIN's ZPD aims to address these issues by providing consumers with more control over their data. This move comes at a crucial time as the industry faces mounting pressure to improve transparency and accountability. The initiative is set to reshape the sector, with experts predicting a significant impact on the growing market.

news.headline: AI is transforming content creation, with users reporting up to 50% time savings. Emplifi survey reveals the impact of AI in this space.
news.summary: Emplifi, a prominent customer engagement platform based in New York, recently unveiled research showcasing the significant role artificial intelligence (AI) plays in content creation. The study revealed that users are experiencing remarkable time savings of up to 50% with the implementation of AI. This substantial decrease in content creation time is proving to be highly advantageous for creators in diverse sectors, enabling them to allocate more attention to strategic endeavors. The survey indicates that a majority of users who incorporate AI into their content creation processes are benefiting from these time efficiencies.

news.headline: Four Interim HealthCare agencies will implement Netsmart CareFabric platform for integrated care delivery.
news.summary: Netsmart, based in Overland Park, Kansas, has revealed that four Interim HealthCare agencies – located in Colorado Springs, Ft. Collins, Pueblo, and Tucson – have opted for the CareFabric platform for facilitating integrated care provision. They will be incorporating myUnity®, a specialized electronic health record (EHR) tailored for post-acute care environments. This EHR system is designed with features such as enhanced user-friendliness, seamless data exchange, accessibility from anywhere, and augmented intelligence technology.

news.headline: Foresight Diagnostics launches SHORTEN-ctDNA trial at Columbia University to optimize treatment duration for DLBCL patients.
news.summary: Foresight Diagnostics, based in Boulder, Colorado, has introduced the SHORTEN-ctDNA clinical trial at Columbia University. This trial, aiming to assess the effectiveness of Foresight CLARITY™ MRD detection technology, targets real-time treatment optimization for diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) patients. Standard treatment for newly diagnosed DLBCL typically involves six cycles of combination therapy.

news.headline: eClinicalWorks + Sunoh.ai help Healing Hands Ministries in Dallas save time on clinical documentation, focus on patient care. #GameChanger.
news.summary: In Westborough, Massachusetts, eClinicalWorks revealed that Healing Hands Ministries (HHM) in Dallas, Texas, a major Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC), has effectively integrated Sunoh.ai into their operations. Sunoh.ai, the primary AI medical scribe in the market, allows clinicians to save time on clinical documentation. This enables physicians to concentrate more on patient care. By being EHR-agnostic, Sunoh.ai drastically cuts down the time spent on clinical documentation, enhancing physician productivity.

news.headline: Rigaku introduces XSPA-200 ER detector for X-ray diffraction system, promises high energy resolution to reduce background noise.
news.summary: A global solution partner in X-ray analytical devices, Rigaku Corporation based in Akishima City, Tokyo, recently announced the launch of their latest product, the XSPA-200 ER. This advanced X-ray seamless pixel array detector has started receiving orders and is designed to combat the rise in background noise from samples by utilizing high energy resolution technology. This feature allows for high-sensitivity measurements, making it suitable for samples with varying compositions and properties. Jun Kawakami, the President and CEO of Rigaku, leads the company as a part of the Rigaku Holdings Group.
